title: 10 best herbal teas for ringworm
intro_desc: Herbal teas for ringworm are infusions made from plants that have antifungal properties, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of ringworm, a common skin infection caused by fungi. Examples of herbal teas that can be beneficial for ringworm include chamomile, calendula, and echinacea. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe itchy and irritated skin, while calendula has antifungal and antiseptic properties that can help to kill the fungus causing the infection. Echinacea, on the other hand, can help to boost the immune system, which can speed up the healing process. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals with ringworm can experience relief from symptoms such as itching, redness, and inflammation, allowing them to go about their daily activities more comfortably.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood teas helps with ringworm because it possesses antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat the fungal infection. The tea's active compounds, such as thujone and camphor, work synergistically to disrupt the growth of the fungus, reducing its ability to spread and cause further damage. Additionally, wormwood's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe itchy skin and reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm, promoting a faster healing process.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Wormwood's antifungal compounds, such as sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids, inhibit the growth of fungal infections like ringworm, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in wormwood tea, including polyphenols and terpenes, help alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with ringworm, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial compounds have been shown to be effective against a range of microorganisms, including fungal and bacterial infections like ringworm, helping to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help combat fungal infections and reduce inflammation.
Bark: Contains tannins and alkaloids that have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective against ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh wormwood leaves or use dried ones in a ratio of one teaspoon per cup boiling water.
Chop the fresh wormwood leaves into small pieces to release their essential oils and flavor compounds.
Heat filtered water to a boil in a saucepan and add the chopped wormwood leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with ringworm because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's flavonoids and saponins work together to reduce fungal infections, such as ringworm, by inhibiting the growth of Candida albicans and other pathogens that cause the infection. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm, providing relief from discomfort and promoting faster healing.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Calendula's antimicrobial compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help to combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth of pathogens.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Calendula tea, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery.

Antifungal Properties: The antifungal properties of Calendula, particularly its calendulic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens responsible for ringworm, helping to clear up infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds, the roots have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help soothe skin irritation caused by ringworm.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, the seeds exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can aid in reducing fungal infections associated with ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried calendula flowers and boiling water for the infusion process.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried petals or three to four tablespoons of fresh petals per cup of boiling water.
Steep the petals in hot water for eight to ten minutes to allow the flavors and oils to infuse.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot to remove solids.
Enjoy the warm, soothing pot marigold tea within 30 minutes of preparation while it is still hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The tea's high concentration of catechins, a type of polyphenol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, such as Trichophyton rubrum and Microsporum canis. Additionally, white tea's flavonoids have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm infections. Drinking or applying topically, white tea teas can provide a natural and effective remedy for this common skin infection.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Herbal white tea teas contain antifungal compounds such as catechins, which can help inhibit the growth and spread of fungal infections like ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants present in herbal white tea teas, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm infections.
Wound-healing properties: Herbal white tea teas contain compounds that promote wound healing and tissue repair, which can aid in the recovery process of skin lesions caused by ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against fungal infections like ringworm.
Bark: The bark contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and heal skin irritations caused by ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of high-quality white tea leaves per 8 ounces of water.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 150 to 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Steep the tea leaves in the hot water for three to five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or fine-mesh sieve.
Allow the tea to cool and infuse for an additional two to three minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with ringworm because it exhibits antimicrobial properties that combat fungal infections, making it a natural remedy for this common skin condition. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and minimizing scarring. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects can calm irritated skin, providing relief from the discomfort and itchiness often experienced during the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: German Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with Ringworm infections, making it more comfortable to treat.
Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial agents can combat the fungus (Trichophyton, Microsporum, or Epidermophyton) that causes Ringworm, helping to clear the infection and promote healing.

Antioxidant properties: German Chamomile's antioxidants can help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by Ringworm, promoting a faster and more effective recovery from the infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing skin irritations and promoting wound healing associated with ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried german chamomile flowers per eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured chamomile with boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes to release the tea's calming properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or infuser and discard the solids.
Serve the herbal tea hot or iced, sweetened with honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory teas helps with ringworm because its antimicrobial properties combat the fungal infection, reducing inflammation and itching. The tea's antioxidants also promote healing by increasing blood flow to the affected area, while its anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm the skin. Additionally, chicory tea has been found to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, making it a natural and effective treatment option for those suffering from this common fungal infection.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Chicory tea's antifungal property helps to combat fungal infections like ringworm, inhibiting the growth and spread of the fungus.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in chicory tea reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery.
Antimicrobial properties: Chicory tea's antimicrobial property helps to prevent bacterial infections that can occur as a secondary infection due to compromised skin caused by ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, chicory roots have been traditionally used to treat fungal infections like ringworm due to their antifungal properties.
Bark: The bark of chicory contains saponins and other phytochemicals that help inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens responsible for ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried chicory leaves and flowers, measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Bring filtered water to a boil in a kettle or pot and let it cool slightly for five minutes.
Pour the hot water over the chicory mixture in a teapot or infuser, allowing steeping for ten to twelve minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids immediately.
Serve the tea hot or chilled, sweetened with honey if desired, and enjoy within two hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with ringworm because of its rich composition of antioxidants, silicon, and other nutrients that promote skin health. The tea's antifungal properties specifically target the fungus causing ringworm, slowing down its growth and reducing the severity of symptoms such as itching, redness, and inflammation. Additionally, horsetail's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and promote healing, accelerating the recovery process and leaving the affected area smooth and healthy.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Horsetail's antimicrobial compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in horsetail tea, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, reduce inflammation and swelling associated with ringworm infections, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Antifungal properties: Horsetail's antifungal compounds, such as silica and potassium, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens like Trichophyton mentagrophytes, which causes ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the horsetail plant are rich in silicates and antioxidants, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Bark: The bark of the horsetail plant contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried horsetail herb for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured horsetail with the boiling water in a medium-sized glass teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to release the active compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Serve the tea hot or allow it to cool to room temperature for immediate consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which target the fungal infections that cause this common skin condition. The tea's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing symptoms like itching, redness, and discoloration. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory effects can help soothe irritated skin, promoting a faster recovery from ringworm infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Fennel tea contains antifungal compounds like anethole and limonene, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, including Trichophyton rubrum.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with ringworm infections, promoting a faster recovery.

Antioxidant activity: Fennel tea's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin damage caused by ringworm, promoting healthy skin regeneration and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and healing skin irritations like ringworm.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain antifungal compounds that can help combat fungal infections like ringworm, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves or use dried fennel powder from 2-4 teaspoons of foeniculum vulgare.
Rinse the fresh fennel leaves with cold water to remove dirt and debris, then chop them into small pieces.
Combine chopped fresh fennel leaves or dried fennel powder with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the herbal essence and flavors into the water.
Strain the tea using a strainer or cheesecloth into a cup, discard solids, and serve warm or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause ringworm, including Trichophyton and Epidermophyton species. Additionally, licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe itchy and irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting faster healing. By targeting both the underlying infection and symptoms, herbal licorice teas may provide a natural and effective solution for treating ringworm.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, which can provide relief to affected individuals.
Liquiritigenin: Exhibits antifungal activity against various pathogens, including those that cause ringworm, which can help to prevent infection spread and promote healing.
Licoricidin: Possesses antimicrobial properties that can combat bacterial infections often secondary to fungal infections like ringworm, promoting a healthy environment for wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to make medicinal teas for ringworm due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have antifungal properties.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes are used in teas for ringworm because they contain flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted source according to local regulations.
Rinse the roots with filtered water to remove any impurities and debris.
Chop 5-10 grams of the cleaned roots into small pieces to release their essential oils.
Combine the chopped roots with 250ml of boiling water in a teapot or infuser and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and serve warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with ringworm because it has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to combat the infection. The active compounds in the tea, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause ringworm. Additionally, St John's wort tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the affected area and reduce symptoms such as redness, itching, and swelling. By drinking this tea regularly, individuals can promote healing and alleviate discomfort associated with ringworm infection.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has antifungal properties, making it effective against fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth and replication of fungal cells.
Hypericin: This pigment has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in areas affected by ringworm infection.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat the underlying infection causing ringworm symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of hypericin, a pigment that has antifungal properties and helps to combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Flowers: The flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat skin conditions, including ringworm, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers from trusted sources.
Rinse the herbal material with clean water to remove dirt and debris then chop finely.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped herb with one cup of boiling water steep for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then drink immediately while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce fungal growth and soothe irritated skin. By promoting a healthy balance of skin flora and reducing inflammation, English lavender tea can help alleviate symptoms like itching, redness, and scaling associated with ringworm, ultimately supporting the body's natural healing process and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: English Lavender's antifungal properties can help combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth and multiplication of fungi, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can soothe skin irritation, redness, and swelling associated with ringworm, reducing discomfort and promoting a faster recovery.
Antioxidant properties: English Lavender's antioxidants help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate ringworm symptoms and delay healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used because they contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm the affected area.
Seeds: Lavender seeds are used in herbal remedies for ringworm due to their antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections causing the condition.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried english lavender flowers according to your preference.
Measure one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers or two tablespoons of fresh flowers per eight ounce cup.
Steep the lavender flowers in boiling water for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool and enjoy within 30 minutes to one hour.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ringworm with herbal teas is by using a strong tea made from antifungal herbs such as calendula, Oregon grape, or goldenseal. Steep the herbs in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then let the tea cool. Apply the tea directly to the affected area using a cotton ball or cloth, allowing it to dry on the skin. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for several weeks, or until the ringworm has fully healed. Remember to wash your hands thoroughly after each application to prevent spreading the infection. Always consult with a healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ringworm that are treated with herbal teas include candidiasis and other fungal infections. Certain herbal teas, such as those made from chamomile, calendula, and echinacea, have antifungal properties that can help to combat these types of infections. Chamomile tea, for example, contains flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, a common cause of fungal infections. Similarly, calendula tea has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe skin irritations and promote healing. Echinacea tea, on the other hand, can help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making it a useful remedy for fungal infections. However, it is important to note that while herbal teas can be helpful in treating fungal infections, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe or persistent cases.
